name: West Tytherely
overview: West Tytherley is a village in the Test Valley on the Hampshire/Wiltshire border (post town Salisbury), about 11 miles east of Salisbury. With neighbouring East Tytherley it forms a pair of Saxon-origin villages - the name derives from the Old English for 'thin or tender wood'. Unusually for its size the village retains a good cluster of amenities, including a primary school, the church of St Peter, a village hall and the 17th-century Black Horse coaching inn.
character: A charming, well-kept Test Valley village with a strong community and a much-loved village pub, ringed by woodland and farmland.
amenities: The Black Horse - a 17th-century Grade II coaching inn with a skittle alley
Church of St Peter and a village hall
A village shop serving the two Tytherleys
Wider shopping in Salisbury, Romsey and Stockbridge

schools.name: West Tytherley CofE Primary School
schools.distance: ~0.5 mile (in the village; federated with Broughton)

commutes.notes: From Dean station, around 2 miles away, on the Salisbury-Southampton line
All within about half an hour, along with Stockbridge and Andover

prices.summary: A sought-after Test Valley village of period cottages and houses; well-regarded for its amenities and setting. Use the SP5 sold-price record for current context.

thingsToDo: The Black Horse pub with its skittle alley at the heart of village life
Walking in the surrounding Test Valley woods and farmland
Mottisfont (National Trust) and the River Test within a short drive
The Clarendon Way and Monarch's Way long-distance paths nearby
pros: Rare for its size in keeping a pub, primary school, church, shop and village hall
Pretty, wooded Test Valley setting with a strong community
Good road links and a nearby station at Dean; several towns within 30 minutes
cons: Further from Salisbury (~11 miles) than the close-in villages
Secondary schooling means travelling to a nearby town
whoItSuits: Families wanting a well-served, pretty Test Valley village with its own school and pub, within reach of Salisbury, Romsey and Winchester.
